title: planchonella howeana
intro: Planchonella howeana, also known as the Howe's rosewood, is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which has been traditionally used in the Pacific region to treat various ailments. The bark of Planchonella howeana contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of Planchonella howeana may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the bark, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
